abstract: Modern scientific experiments increasingly rely on the simultaneous control and monitoring of multiple, heterogeneous data streams. However, because standard computer programming traditionally relies on the serial execution of instructions, developing software that can handle the asynchronous and parallel nature of scientific data remains a significant challenge. To address this, we present Bonsai: a modular, high-performance, open-source visual programming framework designed for the real-time acquisition and processing of diverse data streams. We describe Bonsai’s event-based architecture and core principles, demonstrating how it facilitates the rapid and flexible prototyping of complex experimental designs. We highlight its utility in neuroscience through several practical applications, including high-speed behavioral video tracking, multi-channel electrophysiology, and reactive closed-loop systems for environmental control and stimulation.
